explanations.6: ssh-agent -s outputs shell commands that set up SSH authentication; the eval command runs those commands in the current shell so SSH knows how to find your keys.
explanations.9: The SSH_PRIVATE_KEY variable contains your key in base64 format (a safe text encoding), so we decode it back to binary with base64 -d and save it to the standard SSH key file.
explanations.13: ssh-keyscan connects to the server to grab its public key fingerprint and adds it to known_hosts, which prevents SSH from prompting you to verify the server's identity interactively.
